summary: Official Terraform provider for managing Palantir Foundry platform resources as infrastructure-as-code. Written in Go using the HashiCorp Terraform Plugin Framework, it generates an API client from an OpenAPI v2 spec (gateway-client/v2/api-v2.yml) using oapi-codegen. The provider focuses on Foundry's administrative and organizational primitives -- enrollments, organizations, groups, markings, projects, and spaces -- enabling declarative management of access control, identity, and resource hierarchies. It does NOT manage Ontology-level resources (object types, datasets, link types, etc.) despite those APIs existing in the bundled OpenAPI spec. Authentication uses OAuth2 client_credentials grant via Foundry's Multipass service.

ontologyConcepts.name: Enrollment
ontologyConcepts.description: Top-level administrative container for a Foundry instance. Identified by RID (e.g. ri.control-panel.main.customer.<uuid>). Cannot be created or deleted via Terraform -- only role assignments can be managed. Has enrollment-level role assignments (e.g. enrollment:administrator). Contains organizations, authentication providers, and hosts.

ontologyConcepts.name: Organization
ontologyConcepts.description: A logical grouping within an Enrollment that controls user access and visibility. Has a name, description, host_name, and an associated marking. Cannot be deleted. Organizations gate access to Spaces, Projects, and Groups. Identified by RID.

ontologyConcepts.name: Group
ontologyConcepts.description: A principal (identity) entity that aggregates users or other groups. Has a name, description, realm, and visibility scoped to Organizations. Can be either internally created (AdminCreateGroup) or pre-registered via an external AuthenticationProvider (AdminPreregisterGroup). Identified by UUID. Deletable (soft-delete via API).
relationships: has GroupMembers (principal IDs -- users or groups)

ontologyConcepts.name: GroupMembership
ontologyConcepts.description: A join-table concept: the set of principals (users or groups) that belong to a Group. Managed as a separate Terraform resource from the Group itself to allow independent lifecycle management. Members are identified by principal UUID.
relationships: members are Users or Groups identified by principalId

ontologyConcepts.name: Marking
ontologyConcepts.description: A security classification or access control tag that can be applied to resources. Markings belong to a MarkingCategory (either a user-created UUID category or the built-in 'Organization' category). Markings cannot be deleted. They have role-based access control with three roles: ADMINISTER, DECLASSIFY, and USE.
relationships: has MarkingMembers (principals who can see marked content)
can be applied to Resources (Projects, etc.)

ontologyConcepts.name: MarkingMembership
ontologyConcepts.description: The set of principals (users or groups) who are members of a Marking -- i.e., who can access content tagged with this Marking.

ontologyConcepts.name: MarkingRoleAssignment
ontologyConcepts.description: Role assignments for a Marking. Separate resource for lifecycle management. Roles are ADMINISTER (manage marking), DECLASSIFY (remove marking from resources), USE (apply marking to resources).

ontologyConcepts.name: Space
ontologyConcepts.description: A top-level organizational container for Projects within Foundry's filesystem. Scoped to an Enrollment and gated by Organizations. Has a filesystem, usage account, deletion policy, and a default role set. Deletable.

ontologyConcepts.name: Project
ontologyConcepts.description: A container for resources within a Space. Has role-based access control with resource roles and organization scoping. Supports trash lifecycle (NOT_TRASHED -> DIRECTLY_TRASHED -> permanently deleted). Deletable (two-phase: trash then permanent delete).

ontologyConcepts.name: ProjectOrganizations
ontologyConcepts.description: The set of Organizations that have access to a Project. Managed as a separate resource.

ontologyConcepts.name: ProjectResourceRoles
ontologyConcepts.description: Role assignments for a Project's resources. Principals can be either specific users/groups (principalWithId) or 'everyone'. Managed as a separate resource.
relationships: assigns roles to principals or everyone

ontologyConcepts.name: ProjectMarkings
ontologyConcepts.description: The set of Markings applied to a Project. Managed as a separate resource.

ontologyConcepts.name: Principal
ontologyConcepts.description: Abstract identity concept referenced throughout -- either a User or a Group. Identified by UUID. PrincipalType is USER or GROUP. The 'everyone' pseudo-principal is used for default roles.
relationships: can have RoleAssignments on Enrollments, Organizations, Markings, Projects

ontologyConcepts.name: AuthenticationProvider
ontologyConcepts.description: An identity provider (e.g., SAML) associated with an Enrollment. Used for pre-registering groups before external users log in. Identified by RID. Read-only in this provider (used as reference for group preregistration).

ontologyConcepts.name: ResourceRole
ontologyConcepts.description: A role grant on a filesystem resource (Project). Composed of a principal (either a specific user/group via principalWithId or 'everyone') and a roleId. The principal types are: 'principalWithId' (specific principal with ID and type) and 'everyone' (default role for all).

methods: YAML service discovery file (FOUNDRY_SERVICE_DISCOVERY_V2 env var with multipass and api_gateway keys)

apiSurface.codeGeneration: oapi-codegen v2.5.1 generates Go client (v2.gen.go) from OpenAPI spec

architecturePatterns.name: Terraform Plugin Framework
architecturePatterns.description: Uses hashicorp/terraform-plugin-framework (v1.17.0), not the older SDK. Each resource implements the resource.Resource interface with Configure, Metadata, Schema, Create, Read, Update, Delete methods. Provider type name is 'foundry' (resources are foundry_space, foundry_project, etc.).

architecturePatterns.name: OpenAPI Code Generation
architecturePatterns.description: API client is auto-generated from gateway-client/v2/api-v2.yml using oapi-codegen. The generated client (v2.gen.go) provides typed request/response structs and a ClientWithResponses wrapper. All API interactions go through this generated client.

architecturePatterns.name: Resource Separation Pattern
architecturePatterns.description: Core entities and their sub-resources (membership, role assignments, organizations, markings) are separate Terraform resources with independent lifecycles. For example: foundry_group + foundry_group_membership, foundry_marking + foundry_marking_membership + foundry_marking_role_assignments, foundry_project + foundry_project_organizations + foundry_project_resource_roles + foundry_project_markings.

architecturePatterns.name: Initial-Only Fields
architecturePatterns.description: Some fields are write-once on creation (initial_resource_roles, initial_organizations, initial_administrators, initial_role_assignments). After creation, changes to these are rejected; separate sub-resources must be used instead. This models the Foundry API pattern where creation accepts initial setup but ongoing management uses different endpoints.

architecturePatterns.name: Deletions Disabled Safe Mode
architecturePatterns.description: A provider-level 'deletions_disabled' flag puts the provider in safe mode. When true: deletable resources (space, group, project) are removed from TF state but not deleted remotely; non-deletable resources (enrollment, organization, marking) can be removed from state; membership/role removal operations are skipped with warnings. Designed for environments where IaC may not be the sole source of truth.

architecturePatterns.name: Two-Phase Delete for Projects
architecturePatterns.description: Projects use a two-phase deletion: first trash (FilesystemDeleteResource, sets trash_status=DIRECTLY_TRASHED), then permanent delete (FilesystemPermanentlyDeleteResource). Handles cases where resource may already be trashed (DIRECTLY_TRASHED or ANCESTOR_TRASHED).

architecturePatterns.name: Import State via ID
architecturePatterns.description: All resources implement ImportState, accepting the resource's primary identifier (RID or UUID). The Read method is then called automatically to hydrate the full state from the API.

architecturePatterns.name: Diff-Based Updates
architecturePatterns.description: Membership and role assignment updates compute diffs between old state and new plan, then issue targeted add/remove API calls rather than full replacements. Uses helper.FindStringSliceDiff for simple member lists and custom diff functions for typed role assignments.

architecturePatterns.name: RID vs UUID Identifiers
architecturePatterns.description: Foundry uses two identifier types: RIDs (Resource Identifiers, e.g. ri.control-panel.main.customer.<uuid>) for enrollments, organizations, spaces, projects; and plain UUIDs for groups, markings, users. The provider handles both with appropriate parsing.

architecturePatterns.name: Preview Mode Flag
architecturePatterns.description: Most Admin API endpoints accept a 'preview' boolean query parameter. The provider always sets this to true (constants.PreviewMode = true), indicating these APIs are in preview/beta status.

typeDefinitions.name: FoundryProviderData
typeDefinitions.description: Shared provider configuration passed to all resources. Contains the generated API client and feature flags.

typeDefinitions.name: ResolvedServiceUrls
typeDefinitions.description: Resolved API endpoints for the Foundry instance.

typeDefinitions.name: enrollmentResourceModel
typeDefinitions.description: Terraform state model for Enrollment resource.

typeDefinitions.name: groupResourceModel
typeDefinitions.description: Terraform state model for Group resource.

typeDefinitions.name: groupMembershipResourceModel
typeDefinitions.description: Terraform state model for GroupMembership resource.

typeDefinitions.name: markingResourceModel
typeDefinitions.description: Terraform state model for Marking resource.

typeDefinitions.name: organizationResourceModel
typeDefinitions.description: Terraform state model for Organization resource.

typeDefinitions.name: projectResourceModel
typeDefinitions.description: Terraform state model for Project resource.

typeDefinitions.name: ResourceRole
typeDefinitions.description: A role grant on a Project resource. ResourceRolePrincipal has Type ('principalWithId' or 'everyone'), PrincipalID, and PrincipalType (USER|GROUP).

typeDefinitions.name: spaceResourceModel
typeDefinitions.description: Terraform state model for Space resource.

typeDefinitions.name: CoreRoleAssignmentUpdate
typeDefinitions.description: API type for adding/removing role assignments on enrollments and organizations.

typeDefinitions.name: AdminMarkingRoleUpdate
typeDefinitions.description: API type for adding/removing marking role assignments.

typeDefinitions.name: FilesystemPrincipalWithID
typeDefinitions.description: API type for resource role principals. Type can be 'principalWithId' (specific user/group) or 'everyone'.
